title: Personal Information
shortSummary: Say your name, age, nationality, and where you live so people can get to know you.
culturalNote: Brazilians love to chat and will warmly ask where you are from, often delighted that you are learning their language. Note that Portuguese uses ter (to have) for age: você tem quantos anos? literally asks 'how many years do you have?' Nationalities are adjectives that agree with gender, written in lowercase: a man is australiano, a woman australiana. Saying estou aprendendo português (I'm learning Portuguese) almost always earns encouragement and patience, since Brazilians appreciate the effort enormously.

grammar.explanation: Portuguese uses ter (to have), not ser, for age. You say how many years you 'have'. Tenho vinte anos literally means 'I have twenty years'.
grammar.commonMistake: Saying sou vinte anos with ser. Age always uses tenho with ter.

grammar.explanation: Nationality words are adjectives and change with gender. A man is brasileiro or australiano, a woman is brasileira or australiana. They are written in lowercase.
grammar.commonMistake: Capitalising nationalities or leaving them masculine for a woman.

dialogue.setup: Bia chats with Tom, a traveller she meets at a hostel.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.native: Meu nome é Tom. E você?
lines.english: My name is Tom. And you?

lines.speaker: Bia
lines.native: Eu sou a Bia. De onde você é?
lines.english: I'm Bia. Where are you from?

lines.speaker: Bia
lines.native: Que legal! Quantos anos você tem?
lines.english: How cool! How old are you?

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.native: Tenho vinte e oito anos. Estou aprendendo português.

lines.speaker: Bia
lines.native: Você fala muito bem! Prazer, Tom.
lines.english: You speak really well! Nice to meet you, Tom.

quiz.question: How do you say 'My name is Ana' in Portuguese?
quiz.explanation: Meu nome é means 'my name is'.

quiz.question: Which verb does Portuguese use for age?
quiz.explanation: Tenho trinta anos uses ter, not ser.

quiz.question: _____ em Sydney. (I live)
quiz.explanation: Morar means to live; the eu form is moro.

quiz.question: How would a woman say 'I'm Australian'?
quiz.explanation: Nationality adjectives agree; a woman uses australiana.

quiz.question: Arrange: você / onde / de / é
quiz.explanation: De onde você é? means 'Where are you from?'
